Summary: I like software that makes complicated work easier to reason about. Good systems do not just run; they explain themselves through structure, naming, boundaries, and behavior.
A good tool does more than automate a task. It reduces confusion, makes the next step clearer, and gives people more confidence in the work they are already trying to do.
I enjoy stories that mix structure with ambiguity: comedy that becomes sincere, spectacle with emotional weight, and scenes where small choices change the meaning of everything around them.
Games are systems you can feel. They expose design, feedback, constraints, and consequence in a way that makes abstract engineering ideas immediate.
I am drawn to tools and machines that communicate clearly: old computers, car interiors, physical controls, and interfaces where the design has a point of view.
I like understanding the layers underneath the thing people see: hosting, storage, networking, deployment, backups, and the quiet systems that make software reliable.
